channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Sudan’s civil war: the use of child soldiers and their presence on social media
item.description: The content of the article could not be accessed due to a server restriction, which returned an “Access Denied” message. As a result, a full and accurate rewrite of the original report is not possible...

item.title: UK Commons Speaker Admits Passing Information to Police About Lord Mandelson's Possible Travel Plans Before His Arrest
item.description: Commons Speaker Sir Lindsay Hoyle confirmed in a statement to Parliament that he provided 'relevant' information to the Metropolitan Police regarding Lord Peter Mandelson in good faith, describing it ...

item.title: UK youth unemployment and inactivity approaches one million
item.description: Official figures from the Office for National Statistics indicate that nearly one million young people aged 16 to 24 in the UK were not in education, employment, or training (NEET) between October and...

item.title: Heavy rainfall in Spain and Morocco disrupts UK fruit and vegetable supplies
item.description: UK supermarkets are experiencing sporadic shortages of certain fruits and vegetables due to severe weather conditions in Spain and Morocco, two key suppliers of fresh produce to the British market. St...

item.title: Prince Harry and Meghan Markle visit Jordan to support refugee programs
item.description: The Duke and Duchess of Sussex have arrived in Jordan for a two-day visit aimed at supporting refugees in the region. Their trip began with a roundtable summit hosted by the World Health Organization ...

item.title: Emails show Epstein attributed Prince Andrew’s trade envoy resignation to Charles
item.description: Newly released files from the US Department of Justice reveal that Jeffrey Epstein privately blamed 'Charles' on the day Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor stepped down from his role as the UK’s special repre...

item.title: Billy Porter and Sam Morrison Discuss Their Comedy Show on Love, Grief, and Diabetes
item.description: In an insightful conversation, Billy Porter and Sam Morrison discuss their collaboration on the one-man comedy show *Sugar Daddy*. The show, created by Morrison, is a blend of love, loss, and humour, ...

item.title: Pollster highlights illegal 'family voting' in Gorton and Denton by-election
item.description: Robert Hayward, a leading UK pollster, has voiced serious concerns regarding the conduct of the recent by-election in Gorton and Denton. Observers from Democracy Volunteers reported that illegal 'fami...

item.title: Ian Huntley seriously injured in prison attack at HMP Frankland
item.description: Ian Huntley, convicted of the murders of 10-year-olds Holly Wells and Jessica Chapman in Soham, Cambridgeshire, in 2002, has been seriously injured following an alleged assault in a prison workshop at...

item.title: Understanding and Managing Compulsive Skin Picking Linked to Acne
item.description: In this advice column, a reader asks how to stop compulsively picking at pimples, particularly around their period, despite rejecting unrealistic beauty standards. While they do not mind having acne, ...

item.title: Zimbabwe's Reserve Bank to introduce new ZWG banknotes in April
item.description: The Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe (RBZ) has announced the introduction of new ZWG banknotes, starting with the ZiG10, ZiG20, and ZiG50 denominations, which will begin circulating on April 7, 2026. The ZiG1...

item.title: Can Lynx Be Successfully Reintroduced to Scotland's Highlands Amid Local Concerns?
item.description: Lynx, a wild cat that was driven to extinction in Britain over 1,000 years ago, could be reintroduced to Scotland's forests as part of a conservation effort. While a significant majority of Scots supp...

item.title: Gucci Faces Backlash for Using AI-Generated Images in Milan Fashion Week Promotion
item.description: Gucci has faced criticism for using AI-generated images to promote its upcoming show at Milan Fashion Week. The luxury fashion house shared visuals on social media featuring older Italian women in cla...

item.title: Driver jailed for dangerous driving that led to pregnant woman’s premature birth
item.description: A 30-year-old driver, Jack Bentley from Derby, has been sentenced to two years and four months in prison for causing serious injuries through dangerous driving. On 6 April 2025, Bentley, while gamblin...

item.title: Man Arrested After Defacing Winston Churchill Statue with Pro-Palestinian Graffiti
item.description: A man has been arrested on suspicion of defacing the statue of Sir Winston Churchill in Parliament Square, London. The suspect, aged 38, was detained after spray-painting the bronze statue with red gr...

item.title: Man Sentenced to Prison for Selling Counterfeit Aircraft Engine Parts with Forged Safety Certificates
item.description: Jose Alejandro Zamora Yrala, a 38-year-old former techno DJ, has been sentenced to four years and eight months in prison for selling nearly £7 million worth of counterfeit aircraft engine parts to maj...

item.title: Hillary Clinton’s closed-door Epstein deposition paused after unauthorised photo leak
item.description: Hillary Clinton appeared before the House Oversight Committee for a closed-door deposition as part of its investigation into Jeffrey Epstein’s sex trafficking network and the alleged involvement of pr...

item.title: Ian Huntley taken to hospital after serious assault at HMP Frankland
item.description: Ian Huntley, who is serving a life sentence for the murders of 10-year-olds Holly Wells and Jessica Chapman in 2002, has sustained serious head injuries following an assault at HMP Frankland in County...

item.title: Researchers capture first footage showing virus spillover risk in bat caves
item.description: Scientists have recorded for the first time video evidence highlighting the potential risk of virus spillover from bats to humans. The footage, taken inside a bat cave, shows the natural behaviours an...

item.title: New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani to meet President Trump at the White House despite recent criticism
item.description: President Donald Trump is set to host New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani for a face-to-face meeting at the White House, just days after criticising him during the State of the Union address. During th...
